items.final_wording: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review. This batch also reviews recommendation-qualified upper-GI dysphagia, jaundice, and colorectal rectal-bleeding fields; no cancer diagnosis or automatic referral text is generated. source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

items.rationale: The exact suspected-skin-cancer section supports documenting clinician-confirmed change in size, shape, colour, inflammation, oozing or sensation in a nevus without asserting melanoma or generating referral.

items.final_wording: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

source.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation
source.population: People with a pigmented lesion assessed in secondary or tertiary care and people receiving follow-up after melanoma treatment.
source.setting: Specialist dermatology and skin-cancer assessment and surveillance.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. It does not diagnose melanoma or generate biopsy, imaging, treatment, referral, or follow-up intervals.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

items.rationale: The exact specialist assessment section supports recording dermoscopy, baseline images and interval photographic comparison when actually performed; no diagnosis or excision decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

added_items.rationale: The exact suspected-skin-cancer section supports documenting clinician-confirmed change in size, shape, colour, inflammation, oozing or sensation in a nevus without asserting melanoma or generating referral.

added_items.final_wording: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

source.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation
source.population: People with a pigmented lesion assessed in secondary or tertiary care and people receiving follow-up after melanoma treatment.
source.setting: Specialist dermatology and skin-cancer assessment and surveillance.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. It does not diagnose melanoma or generate biopsy, imaging, treatment, referral, or follow-up intervals.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

added_items.rationale: The exact specialist assessment section supports recording dermoscopy, baseline images and interval photographic comparison when actually performed; no diagnosis or excision decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review.
